carbon_policy_signal.item: EU methane rule exemptions for energy security emerging; signals willingness to loosen upstream supply discipline under geopolitical crisis.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Supply loosening reduces regulatory carbon floor on gas availability; neutral near-term EUA pressure but structural tightening of commodity basis (gas per tonne CO2) as thermal dispatch extends.

freshness_caveat: Coal (132 days stale, as of 2025-12-26) and Clean Dark (127 days stale, as of 2025-12-31) are not actionable; intra-week and daily moves in Clean Spark (+78.55% W/W, 92nd pctile) and Renewables (−41.24% W/W, 18th pctile) dominate signal. TTF, EUA, DE Power, GB Power, and Renewables are fresh (<2 days old).
risk_flags: Coal and Clean Dark metrics stale >127 days; intra-week readings (Clean Spark +78.55% W/W, Renewables −41.24% W/W) dominate live signal.
Renewables at 18th percentile (27.07% of load, −32.82% daily) with −15.03% monthly collapse; thermal baseload callable indefinitely.
Hormuz blockade ongoing; LNG decouple from TTF into Asia raises EU import cost; no resolution timeline stated—horizon weeks to months.
Belgian nuclear takeover under review; if executed Cal+1 gas demand structurally lower, but timing uncertain.

base.summary: Hormuz blockade persists; renewables stabilize ~25–30%; thermal baseload extended; TTF mid-40s EUR/MWh.

downside.summary: Hormuz blockade escalates (military action) or widens; renewables extend below 20%; sustained LNG shortage.

upside.summary: Hormuz clears (Trump mission success) or Iran deal emerges; LNG supply normalizes; Asian-EU spread collapses.

themes: Geopolitical LNG supply shock via Strait of Hormuz
Renewables collapse driving thermal dispatch (27% → 18th pctile weekly)
Clean Spark elevated; Coal at historical lows; fuel-switch regime firm
Belgian nuclear policy bearish for gas curve; tariff-inflation risk looming
top_drivers: Hormuz closure raises LNG cost to EU; TTF bullish despite −6.44% daily (baseline elevated relative to 57th pctile); gas-for-power switching sustains Clean Spark at 92nd pctile.
Renewables collapse (18th pctile, −41.24% weekly) forces thermal dispatch; coal at 8th pctile but stale—live signal is Clean Spark +78.55% W/W margin extension.
Belgian nuclear policy (months) + methane rule relaxation (weeks) signal EU accepting structural gas/thermal dependence under geopolitical pressure; bearish Cal+1, bullish prompt thermal.
top_takeaway: Hormuz closure pushes LNG/gas bullish while renewables plunge (18th pctile); Clean Spark at 92nd pctile signals sustained thermal in-the-money—but coal data stale 132 days.

watchlist.why: Direct transmission to LNG affordability and TTF via arbitrage; EU power dispatch sensitivity ±5–10% if 4–6 week disruption.
At 18th pctile; any further decline locks thermal premium; upside reversal critical to Cool Spark disinflation.
Announced months-horizon; structural gas demand reduction if executed; gas curve Cal+1 downside 8–12% over 12–18m.
Shipping clearance by end-May could ease crude/LNG flow; if achieved, LNG premium collapses, TTF −5–8%, DE Power −3–5%.
Inflation spillover into energy transition capex; indirect pressure on renewable supply-chain costs; policy uncertainty brake on decarbonization capex.

watchlist_dated.name: Trump Hormuz mission update expected
watchlist_dated.why: Shipping clearance milestone could signal LNG flow restoration; material for TTF/power curve repricing within 48h.
